description_long: This object is a long, slender sword with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a fine point. The blade is dark, likely made of steel or a similar metal, and appears to be well-polished with a subtle reflective sheen. The hilt features a prominent crossguard with ornate, gold-toned detailing, and the handle is ergonomically shaped, possibly wrapped or textured for grip, ending in a rounded pommel. The crossguard and pommel both display decorative flourishes, suggesting a ceremonial or high-status weapon. The overall design is reminiscent of European medieval swords, combining both functionality and aesthetic appeal. No sheath or scabbard is present in the images.
description: A long, straight, double-edged sword with a dark blade and ornate gold-toned crossguard and pommel, featuring decorative details and an ergonomic handle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the width of the blade and the ornate crossguard and pommel.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the full length and slenderness of the blade, with the handle and crossguard visible in silhouette.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the blade and the decorative crossguard and pommel details.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the blade's length and the handle's ergonomic curve.
attribute_handle_design: curved, gold-toned, possibly wrapped or textured, rounded pommel
attribute_ornamentation: ornate gold-toned crossguard and pommel with decorative flourishes
